mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates expanding labor income and consumption capacity, which translates into higher expected corporate revenues and earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not positive in at least 55% of months when PAYEMS increases month-over-month, or the Rank IC falls below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate at or above 2.25% represents restrictive monetary policy that raises discount rates and debt service costs for corporations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 50% of trading weeks when FEDFUNDS >= 2.25%, or the mean return in those periods is not statistically below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a tight labor market, measured by an unemployment rate below 4.0%, and restrictive monetary policy, measured by a federal funds rate above 2.0%, jointly signals an overheating economy vulnerable to a hard landing; this interaction predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition does not produce negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in at least 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction term in a bivariate regression lacks statistical significance at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month declines in industrial production (INDPRO) predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days when monetary policy is in an easing cycle, defined as a stable or declining federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS), because markets front-run additional accommodation.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS stable or declining over prior 3 months and INDPRO declines month-over-month
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is stable or declining, INDPRO month-over-month declines do not coincide with positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in at least 50% of instances, or the conditional Sharpe ratio is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) exceeding 1,200 thousand units indicate robust demand in interest-rate-sensitive durable goods and construction sectors, translating into positive earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in fewer than 55% of months when HOUST exceeds 1,200, or the mean excess return in those months is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) reduces real corporate margins and increases the probability of tighter future monetary policy, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month increases in CPIAUCSL do not correlate with negative 20-day S&P 500 returns (Rank IC > -0.03) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) of more than 0.2 percentage points over a three-month window signals weakening labor demand that precedes declines in personal consumption expenditures, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 55% of instances when UNRATE rises more than 0.2 percentage points over three months.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) directly tracks aggregate consumer spending; positive changes in RSAFS lead to upward earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in RSAFS do not coincide with positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of observations, or the information coefficient is below 0.03.
